result:
The spec is implemented and the project builds cleanly with no warnings.

I added the three missing operations to `CsvRow.cs`, following the existing static-class style:

- **`EscapeField`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it wraps it in double-quotes with internal quotes doubled.
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; an empty list yields the empty string via `string.Join`.
- **`SplitRow`** — a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (doubled quotes contribute one literal `"`, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. After each field, a comma continues the loop (so a trailing comma produces a final empty field) and end-of-line stops it. The empty line naturally parses to a single empty field, matching the spec's round-trip guarantee for all non-empty field lists.

`NeedsQuoting` was left untouched, and no test or project files were changed. One build cycle used out of the budget of ten.
